text: BIG: Hezbollah launched a shore-to-sea missile at a British warship approximately 70 nautical miles off the coast of Lebanon, after apparently mistaking it for an Israeli vessel. Israeli assessments indicate that the ship sustained damage. Source: N14
🟡 Footage of Hezbollah Targeting Israeli Army Humvee with Loitering Drone Near Mays al-Jabal Hospital

NEW: Regional countries including Türkiye, Egypt, and Pakistan are trying to bring Iran to negotiations with the U.S. to stop the war or secure a temporary cease-fire, but efforts have stalled. Iran has rejected proposals, including one to reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for a cease-fire, and declined meeting U.S. officials. Source: WSJ

The IDF said Hezbollah "systematically…" ⚡️ 🇺🇳 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 UNIFIL spokesperson Kandice Ardiel warned that ongoing exchanges of fire by Israeli forces and Hezbollah near its positions are placing peacekeepers at serious risk, following recent incidents of casualties among UN personnel. She accused both sides of firing projectiles and conducting operations in close proximity to UN positions, raising concerns that such actions could provoke return fire and further endanger peacekeepers. UNIFIL urged all parties to respect the safety and inviolability of United Nations premises and personnel, and called for an immediate move toward a ceasefire. @wfwitness

🇦🇪 🇬🇧 — Middle East Eye: A British flight attendant was arrested in the UAE after sharing a photo of a drone airstrike, highlighting an increasing crackdown on people documenting the war and raising questions about Dubai’s image as an open global hub. – A 25-year-old cabin crew member was detained after posting a photo of damage near Dubai International Airport in a private WhatsApp group. 🔴 @alsaa_plus_EN

🇮🇷 Tehran Prosecutor conducts a field inspection of food markets on Day 36 of the war: "No shortage of essential goods was observed. Rice prices unchanged. No shortage of meat, poultry, or fish. Reserves are in good condition." One shopper tells the reporter: "Actually better than before the war." Since Day 1 of what Iran calls the Third Imposed War, the judiciary has been tasked with special market oversight, with hotlines for reporting price gouging or supply refusals. The sanctions were supposed to break Iran, yet the shelves are full. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

Israel sees a low chance of a deal with Iran and is preparing for potential major strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure, including key and newly built facilities. Source: Israeli Channel 14

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 The IDF reports that they eliminated Mohammadreza Ashrafi-Ghehi, head of commercial affairs for the IRGC’s Oil Headquarters, in an airstrike in the Tehran area. The Oil Headquarters is described as a key institude within the IRGC structure, responsible for generating revenue through oil sales and bypass sanctions, which Israel says helps fund military capabilities and regional proxy groups. @wfwitness

Open Source Intel Israel is reportedly planning a wave of strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure, including “significant” targets, as officials assess chances of a deal with Tehran remain low. tweet

↩️ Reply to OSINT Visioner 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 🇮🇱 BIG | Israeli Channel 12: New details have emerged about the rescue of the American F‑15E navigator shot down over Iran. The officer was unconscious with a concussion and did not transmit a distress signal initially. He made first contact at 12:00 on Friday, climbed to the highest point to avoid detection, walked 10‑12 kilometres and hid in a crevice, from where he sent precise coordinates on Friday night. Israel refrained from striking in the area and provided intelligence. During Friday and Saturday, Israel was asked to assist with air superiority and struck relevant targets. U.S. forces seized a farm 18 kilometres from his hideout, landing two aircraft and small helicopters. The helicopters extracted him from the crevice and returned him to the farm. During takeoff, two C‑130s became stuck in sand; three smaller planes were called to evacuate the navigator and special forces. Fighter jets then bombed the two stranded aircraft to prevent capture. See the latest updates with us: @visionergeo tweet

CENTCOM additionally confirms the successful recovery of both the Pilot and Weapons System Officer (WSO) in two separate search-and-rescue operations in Iran. tweet
